content:
A Magistrate can grant bail in a non-bailable offence only if there is no reasonable ground to believe that the accused is guilty of an offence punishable with:
1.​Death, or
2.​Life imprisonment.
.
.
.
.
Note: No material/information provided here should be construed as legal advice.

comment: At practice level, the "no reasonable ground" test often hinges on FIR/case diary at early stage, risking a mini-trial; Art 21 liberty vs investigation balance becomes critical.
